qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: srcset attribute XSS bypass allows javascript: URLs to bypass sanitization
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause: this finding concerns srcset being allowed by html_sanitizer.ts without URI_ATTRS sanitization, while the advisory concerns i18n-<attribute> handling bypassing sanitization for security-sensitive bound attributes.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded template parser depth allows DoS through deeply nested HTML
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause and impact: this finding concerns unbounded template parser nesting or ICU parsing causing denial of service, not i18n-marked sensitive attribute bindings bypassing Angular URL sanitization.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: APP_ID injection in SSR allows server-side XSS
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause: this finding concerns platform-server APP_ID embedding in generated SSR JavaScript, while the advisory concerns compiler/runtime i18n attribute metadata causing sanitizer bypass for sensitive attributes such as href.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described Angular i18n attribute binding sanitizer bypass. The retained qualifying findings address srcset sanitizer classification, template parser denial of service, and SSR APP_ID JavaScript embedding instead.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-g93w-mfhg-p222 describes XSS when a security-sensitive bound attribute such as href is also marked with i18n-<attribute>, bypassing Angular sanitization.
vuln-1-srcset-xss-bypass reports srcset being present in VALID_ATTRS but absent from URI_ATTRS in packages/core/src/sanitization/html_sanitizer.ts.
vuln-2-template-parser-dos reports unbounded parser depth and ICU loop behavior in packages/compiler/src/ml_parser/parser.ts.
vuln-3-ssr-appid-injection reports unescaped APP_ID interpolation in packages/platform-server/src/utils.ts.
